name: barbell overhead squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell with a wide grip, positioning it overhead with your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. Hold the barbell with a wide grip, positioning it overhead with your arms fully extended. Engage your core and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Axial loading under fatigue may worsen asymmetrical curves. Prefer split-stance or supported variations.
Overhead positions can stress shoulder and elbow joints. Reduce ROM and load as needed.
Loaded spinal flexion or heavy axial loading should be avoided. Use supported variations.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Avoid pushing into end-range positions. Prioritize midrange control and joint stability over depth.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
